title: Buttery Fluffy Kenyan Chicken Pilaf
description: A diabetic-friendly, buttery, and fluffy Kenyan dish perfect for a date night, featuring scallions and reduced sugar content. This recipe combines the flavors of Kenya with a healthy twist.
instructions: Rinse the rice in a fine-mesh strainer and drain well.
In a large saucepan, melt 1 tablespoon of butter over medium heat.
Add the chopped scallions and cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the chicken to the saucepan and cook until browned, about 5-6 minutes.
Add the cumin, coriander, salt, and pepper to the saucepan and stir to combine.
Add the rice to the saucepan and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add the chicken broth to the saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20-25 minutes or until the liquid is absorbed and the rice is tender.
Fluff the pilaf with a fork and stir in the remaining 1 tablespoon of butter until melted.
Garnish with chopped parsley and serve hot.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
